title: Fix LaTeX data integrity errors and implement automated validation
description: main.tex still contains incorrect CMI statistics from a different analysis partition: absorbed mean CMI=0.687 (should be 0.649), U=41.0 (should be 28.0), p=0.042 (should be 0.045). These appear in Abstract, Introduction, Section 6.2, and Figure 4 caption. This is the 6th consecutive iteration with data pipeline integrity failures. The root cause -- no automated cross-check between paper text and source JSONs -- has been identified since iteration 1 but never addressed.
action: IMMEDIATE: search-and-replace in main.tex: 0.687->0.649, U=41.0->U=28.0, p=0.042->p=0.045. STRUCTURAL: implement validate_integration.py that extracts numbers from paper.md/main.tex and cross-checks against source JSON fields. Run before every paper revision. Standardize on one canonical vocabulary (1,196-word confound decomposition set) and recompute all analyses consistently.

title: Downgrade theoretical overclaiming throughout the paper
description: Section 6 title says 'CMI Predicts Absorption Susceptibility' from rho=-0.383 (p=0.059 uncorrected, p=0.236 Bonferroni-corrected) that reverses at d'>=20. Abstract says 'predicts.' Introduction says 'first information-theoretic criterion.' Phase transition scale match (10.2% error) is partly circular. This is the 3rd consecutive iteration with overclaiming.
action: Replace: 'predicts' -> 'shows directionally correct negative association with'; Section 6 title -> 'Suggestive CMI-Absorption Association at d'=10'; 'first information-theoretic criterion' -> 'first step toward an information-theoretic diagnostic'; remove '10.2% relative error' claim or label as tautological. Always pair uncorrected p (0.059) with Bonferroni-corrected p (0.236) in same sentence in abstract/introduction/conclusion.

title: Execute activation patching on 9 persistent core words
description: The 9 words (eight, lower, liked, offer, often + 4 unnamed) are the paper's only candidates for genuine competitive exclusion. Without activation patching (zero child feature, check parent recovery), this classification is observational. This is the single highest-information-gain experiment: positive results confirm competitive exclusion at small scale; negative results strengthen hedging-only narrative. Either outcome improves the paper.
action: For each of 9 words: (1) identify child feature from confound_decomposition_multi_l0.json absorbing_features, (2) run model forward pass with and without zeroing the child feature's activation, (3) check if parent-associated latent fires when child is zeroed. Report per-word recovery rate. If 7+/9 recover: genuine competitive exclusion confirmed. If <3/9 recover: hedging-only narrative strengthened.

title: Tighten hedging classification to check parent-specific resolution
description: Current classification: any FN resolving at any higher L0 = hedging. At L0=176, 99.16% of tokens resolve trivially. The 98.6% hedging figure is an upper bound including compensatory activation and probe behavior changes, not just genuine hedging. The classification checks whether the TOKEN stops being a FN, not whether the SPECIFIC PARENT LATENT fires.
action: For each of 648 hedging tokens at L0=22: check whether the specific parent-associated latent (highest cosine to probe direction, cosine >= 0.025) has non-zero activation at L0=176. Reclassify: 'strict hedging' = parent latent fires at higher L0; 'compensatory resolution' = token resolves but parent latent does not fire. Report both permissive and strict classifications side by side.

title: Control for probe quality confound in CMI analysis
description: Probe F1 correlates with absorption rate at rho=-0.67 (p<0.001). CMI analysis uses rates from L0=82 where 15/25 letters fail the F1>0.85 quality gate. The CMI-absorption correlation may be mediated by probe quality rather than reflecting a genuine information-theoretic relationship.
action: Three zero-GPU-cost analyses: (1) Compute partial Spearman correlation CMI vs absorption controlling for probe F1. (2) Restrict CMI analysis to 10 quality-gated letters (F1>0.85), report subset correlation. (3) Compute CMI-absorption correlation using L0=22 absorption rates where all probes have F1=1.0 (from confound_decomposition_multi_l0.json). Report all three alongside the full-sample result.

title: Report threshold sensitivity results and diagnose control failure
description: ablation_threshold_sensitivity.json (141KB) was computed but never reported. This directly tests the metric miscalibration argument. Additionally, no mechanistic diagnosis of WHY controls fail is provided.
action: Two tasks: (1) Extract key findings from ablation_threshold_sensitivity.json and include in paper (main text if thresholds fix the control failure; appendix if they don't). Report CV across threshold grid and whether any threshold combination produces measured > shuffled. (2) Compute expected shuffled absorption rate analytically from cosine distribution of 16,384 decoder columns vs random directions. If >90% of random directions find candidate features at tau_cos=0.025, the explanation is feature geometry + permissive threshold.

title: CMI replication at L0=22 with pre-registered d'=10
description: At L0=22, all probes have F1=1.0 (eliminates probe quality confound) and absorption variance is maximal (0% to 66.1%). This is the maximally favorable condition for the CMI analysis. The current result uses L0=82 rates where the probe quality confound is active. Pre-registering d'=10 resolves the dimension selection concern.
action: Recompute CMI for all 25 letters using L0=22 absorption rates (from confound_decomposition_multi_l0.json). The CMI estimates themselves may not change (computed once from activations), but the absorbed/non-absorbed partition will change because L0=22 has different rates. Report Spearman rho, Mann-Whitney, Cohen's d using L0=22 rates with d'=10 pre-registered as primary.

title: Leave-one-out sensitivity analysis for CMI correlation
description: Letter S has CMI=0.961 (high) but absorption=31.43% (high) -- a clear outlier contradicting the claimed pattern. Letter K has CMI=0.592 (low) but absorption=0%. With n=25 and marginal significance, these outliers may dominate the result.
action: For each of 25 letters, compute Spearman rho with that letter removed. Report: which letters, if removed, change sign or significance. Discuss S and K explicitly as exceptions. Investigate: S has n=70 words (largest), K has n=25 (small). Does sample size per letter affect CMI estimation reliability?

title: Reframe cross-domain novelty claim
description: Paper claims 'first absorption measurements on geographic, linguistic, and taxonomic hierarchies' while admitting 'rates cannot be interpreted as genuine absorption.' This is a self-contradiction.
action: Reframe: novelty is 'first cross-architecture validation of the Chanin metric on JumpReLU SAEs, demonstrating universal control failure across five hierarchy domains.' Cross-domain experiments are the validation vehicle, not an independent contribution.

title: Fix structural redundancy and compress confounded comparison
description: Control failure stated 4 times (Introduction, Section 4.1, post-Table-2, Section 4.4). Section 5.3 (JumpReLU vs L1-ReLU, ~200 words) presents confounded data with formal statistical tests.
action: Reduce to 2 statements (Introduction + Section 4.1). Compress Section 5.3 to 2-3 sentences in Discussion. Use recovered space for threshold sensitivity results or method diagram.

title: Explicitly articulate both interpretations of the central finding
description: The paper favors 'metric miscalibrated for JumpReLU' throughout, but 'JumpReLU genuinely has minimal absorption' is equally consistent with the data. Without activation patching, these cannot be distinguished.
action: Add a paragraph in Section 7.2 articulating both interpretations with neutral language. Replace 'miscalibrated for JumpReLU feature geometry' with 'the metric's output is inconsistent with hierarchy-driven absorption on JumpReLU SAEs.' Frame activation patching as the discriminating test.

title: Add CMI estimation diagnostics
description: k-NN MI estimator (k=5, n=3000, d'=10) with no reported diagnostics: no bootstrap CIs per letter, no convergence curves, no k-sensitivity, no alternative estimator comparison. The sign reversal at d'>=20 may reflect estimator bias.
action: Report: (1) bootstrap standard errors for each letter's CMI (10k resamples), (2) convergence curves (CMI vs sample size) for 3-5 representative letters, (3) k sensitivity (k=3,5,10,20). If bootstrap SEs are large relative to group mean difference (0.212), the Mann-Whitney test may be driven by outlier letters.

title: Fix notation and companion document inconsistencies
description: outline.md line 118 has inverted hedging/hierarchy-driven definitions. Variable alpha used without definition in Section 3.5. Population vs sample std ambiguity (0.187 vs 0.195).
action: Fix outline.md definitions. Add 'absorption rate alpha' definition on first use. Decide on std convention (recommend sample std) and apply consistently.

title: Improve title and abstract
description: Title 'Beyond Competitive Exclusion' implies disproven competitive exclusion; the paper actually shows the Chanin metric conflates hedging with competitive exclusion. Abstract at ~290 words with too many details including geometric constant (no predictive value).
action: Consider title: 'Auditing Feature Absorption: Hedging Artifacts Dominate the Chanin Metric on JumpReLU SAEs.' Trim abstract to 220 words: cut geometric constant sentence, replace per-domain numbers with 'all five tested domains,' focus on three pillars.

title: Name all 9 persistent core words and add method diagram
description: Only 5 of 9 persistent core words named. Source data lists only 5 in hierarchy_details. A NeurIPS paper needs a method diagram.
action: Run cross-L0 persistence check on full per-word data to identify and name all 9 words. Create pipeline schematic figure (probe training -> feature identification -> absorption classification -> confound decomposition).
